question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the preamble of the SEBI Act, 1992?

answer.text: To regulate banking activities and protect depositors
To promote foreign investment and regulate currency exchange
To protect the interests of investors in securities, promote the development of, and regulate the securities market
To establish stock exchanges and register all financial intermediaries

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The preamble of the SEBI Act states three objectives: (1) protect interests of investors in securities, (2) promote the development of the securities market, and (3) regulate the securities market. These three pillars guide all SEBI actions. **The Trap:** Regulating banking is the domain of RBI, not SEBI. Promoting foreign investment and regulating currency exchange falls under RBI and FEMA. Establishing stock exchanges is only one of many SEBI functions, not its stated preamble. **Cross-Exam Context:** The SEBI preamble is fundamental to all NISM certifications and frequently tested as a foundational concept. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the three pillars: Protect, Promote, Regulate. Every SEBI regulation can be traced back to one or more of these objectives.
